title: Data Scientist
overview: Data Scientists analyze and interpret complex data to help companies make decisions.
work_profile: Collecting, cleaning, and analyzing datasets; building predictive models.
industry: High demand across IT, healthcare, finance, e-commerce.
education: B.Tech / B.Sc in CS, Stats, or Math
future_scope: AI and ML are growing, data jobs projected to rise by 30% in 10 years.
day_in_life: Morning stand-up meeting → Data cleaning → Model building → Team collaboration.
success_stories: Dr. DJ Patil (Former US Chief Data Scientist)
faqs: Q: Do I need a PhD? → Not mandatory, but helps.
Q: Is coding required? → Yes, Python/SQL.

title: Engineering
overview: Engineers design, build, and maintain systems, structures, machines, and technologies across industries.
work_profile: Problem-solving, designing solutions, research, project management, and applying scientific principles.
industry: IT, manufacturing, automotive, civil infrastructure, electronics, aerospace.
roadmap: Jobs → Junior Engineer → Engineer → Senior Engineer
future_scope: Engineering will remain critical with AI, robotics, renewable energy, and smart infrastructure growth.
day_in_life: Team meetings → Designing systems → Simulations → Documentation → Client presentations.
faqs: Q: Which branch is best? → Depends on your interest and market demand.
Q: Do all engineers need coding? → Not all, but helpful.

title: Doctor
overview: Doctors diagnose, treat, and prevent illnesses, ensuring the well-being of patients.
work_profile: Examining patients, prescribing medicines, performing surgeries, medical research.
industry: Healthcare, hospitals, research institutes, private practice.
future_scope: Healthcare will continue growing with demand for specialists and telemedicine.
day_in_life: Patient consultations → Diagnosis → Prescribing treatment → Surgeries (if specialist) → Ward visits.
faqs: Q: Is MBBS mandatory? → Yes for doctors in India.
Q: Can I work abroad after MBBS? → Yes, after licensing exams like USMLE, PLAB.

title: Pharmacist
overview: Pharmacists prepare and dispense medicines, ensuring their safe and effective use.
work_profile: Dispensing medicines, advising patients, conducting research on drug formulations.
industry: Healthcare, pharmaceuticals, biotech companies, research labs.
future_scope: Pharma industry is growing with personalized medicine and biotechnology.
day_in_life: Morning shift at pharmacy → Advising patients → Coordinating with doctors → Record keeping.
faqs: Q: Is NEET required? → No, for Pharmacy NEET is not required.
Q: Can pharmacists open their own store? → Yes, after license.

title: Director
overview: Film and theatre directors oversee the creative process of movies, shows, or performances.
work_profile: Script selection, directing actors, managing production, editing supervision.
industry: Film industry, OTT platforms, theatre, media production.
future_scope: OTT boom and global content demand is creating more opportunities.
day_in_life: Script reading → Actor rehearsals → Shooting supervision → Editing checks.
faqs: Q: Is a degree compulsory? → No, but formal training helps.
Q: How do I become a director? → Start with short films and assist professionals.

title: Journalism
overview: Journalists gather, assess, and present news and information to the public.
work_profile: Reporting, interviewing, editing, writing articles, anchoring shows.
industry: News media, TV, radio, digital journalism, publishing.
future_scope: Digital journalism and independent media are booming.
day_in_life: Morning briefing → Research stories → Interviews → Writing/editing → Broadcasting.
faqs: Q: Do I need English proficiency? → Strong language skills are crucial.
Q: Is journalism safe? → Depends on the beat and region.

title: Business
overview: Business professionals manage, organize, and grow companies or start their own ventures.
work_profile: Managing teams, analyzing markets, making financial decisions, driving growth.
pros: Option to start your own business
future_scope: Startups and digital businesses are growing rapidly.
day_in_life: Team meetings → Strategy discussions → Financial planning → Client interactions.
faqs: Q: Is MBA compulsory? → Not mandatory, but highly recommended.
Q: Can I start a business after 12th? → Yes, with knowledge and capital.

title: Software Engineer
overview: Software Engineers design, develop, and maintain software systems and applications.
work_profile: Writing code, debugging, collaborating with teams, testing, deployment.
roadmap: Jobs → Developer → Engineer → Senior Engineer → Architect
future_scope: AI, cybersecurity, and cloud computing are creating huge opportunities.
day_in_life: Daily standup → Coding → Debugging → Team reviews → Deployment.
faqs: Q: Do I need CS in 12th? → Helpful but not mandatory.
Q: Is coding compulsory? → Yes, coding is core.

title: Lawyer
overview: Lawyers represent clients in legal matters, provide legal advice, and uphold justice.
work_profile: Researching laws, drafting legal documents, arguing in courts, client consultations.
industry: Law firms, judiciary, corporate sector, government.
roadmap: Jobs → Junior Lawyer → Advocate → Senior Counsel/Judge
future_scope: Rising demand for legal professionals in business, cyber law, and international law.
day_in_life: Court hearings, client meetings, drafting contracts, legal research.
faqs: Q: Is CLAT required? → Yes for top NLUs.
Q: Can lawyers practice abroad? → With local licenses.

title: Teacher/Professor
overview: Teachers and professors educate students, conduct research, and contribute to academic growth.
work_profile: Teaching, preparing lessons, mentoring, academic research.
future_scope: EdTech and global education expansion create new opportunities.
day_in_life: Preparing lectures, teaching, student mentoring, grading papers.
faqs: Q: Do professors need a PhD? → Yes in universities.
Q: Can teachers freelance? → Yes, via EdTech.

title: Pilot
overview: Pilots operate aircraft to transport passengers or cargo safely.
work_profile: Flight operations, navigation, pre-flight checks, safety compliance.
future_scope: Rising global demand for pilots with airline industry growth.
day_in_life: Flight briefings, flying, monitoring systems, post-flight reports.
faqs: Q: Is eyesight important? → Yes, strict medical fitness required.
Q: Can pilots switch airlines? → Yes, with valid licenses.

title: Chartered Accountant (CA)
overview: CAs handle finance, taxation, auditing, and business advisory for individuals and organizations.
work_profile: Auditing accounts, managing taxation, financial planning, compliance.
roadmap: Jobs → Audit Associate → Senior CA → CFO
future_scope: Global demand for CAs with international business expansion.
day_in_life: Financial audits, tax filing, compliance meetings, consulting.
faqs: Q: Is math compulsory? → Not for CA but helpful.
Q: Can CA work abroad? → Yes with equivalent recognition.

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
overview: Cybersecurity Analysts protect systems and data from cyber threats and attacks.
work_profile: Monitoring security systems, identifying vulnerabilities, responding to threats.
roadmap: Jobs → Security Analyst → Cybersecurity Engineer → CISO
future_scope: Cybersecurity demand will rise globally with digitalization and AI.
day_in_life: Monitoring alerts, running penetration tests, incident response.
faqs: Q: Is coding needed? → Yes, basics of Python/C required.
Q: Can I work remotely? → Yes, in many cases.

title: AI Engineer
overview: AI Engineers build intelligent systems using machine learning and deep learning models.
work_profile: Designing AI models, training algorithms, deploying AI solutions.
roadmap: Jobs → AI Engineer → Senior AI Engineer → AI Scientist
future_scope: AI is booming, job demand expected to double in the next decade.
day_in_life: Model training, code debugging, data analysis, team collaboration.
faqs: Q: Is PhD required? → Not mandatory.
Q: Do AI engineers need math? → Yes, linear algebra & statistics.

title: Graphic Designer
overview: Graphic Designers create visual content for branding, marketing, and communication.
work_profile: Designing logos, ads, UI/UX, social media content.
roadmap: Jobs → Junior Designer → Senior Designer → Art Director
future_scope: Design demand will rise with digital transformation.
day_in_life: Client briefs, designing, feedback, revisions.
faqs: Q: Is coding required? → Not for basic design.
Q: Can designers freelance? → Yes.

title: Civil Services (IAS/IPS)
overview: Civil Servants administer public policies, maintain law and order, and serve the nation.
work_profile: Policy implementation, governance, law enforcement, public administration.
roadmap: UPSC Prep → Civil Services Exam → IAS/IPS
future_scope: Civil services remain prestigious with long-term stability.
day_in_life: Meetings with officials, inspections, policy reviews, law enforcement.
faqs: Q: Is coaching mandatory? → No but helpful.
Q: Can engineers become IAS? → Yes, any graduate can.

title: Nurse
overview: Nurses provide medical care, emotional support, and assistance to doctors in healthcare settings.
work_profile: Patient care, administering medication, monitoring health conditions.
roadmap: Jobs → Staff Nurse → Senior Nurse → Nursing Superintendent
future_scope: Nursing demand growing with global healthcare expansion.
day_in_life: Monitoring patients, administering medicines, assisting doctors.
faqs: Q: Can nurses work abroad? → Yes, after licensing exams.
Q: Is NEET required? → No, only for MBBS.

title: Entrepreneur / Startup Founder
overview: Entrepreneurs build businesses, innovate solutions, and create value in society.
work_profile: Idea generation, business strategy, fundraising, product development.
industry: All industries – tech, finance, healthcare, retail.
cost: Varies (₹5 Lakhs - Crores, depending on business)
future_scope: Entrepreneurship is booming globally, supported by government and investors.
day_in_life: Meetings, fundraising, product development, team management.
faqs: Q: Do I need MBA? → Not mandatory.
Q: What is the risk? → High but rewarding.

title: Architect
overview: Architects design buildings and structures ensuring functionality, safety, and aesthetics.
work_profile: Designing blueprints, using CAD software, supervising construction, meeting clients.
industry: Real estate, construction, interior design, urban planning.
roadmap: Internships → Architect → Senior Architect → Urban Planner
future_scope: Sustainable and smart city planning is boosting demand for architects.
day_in_life: Client meetings, designing, site visits, supervising construction.
faqs: Q: Is B.Arch 5 years? → Yes, including internships.
Q: Do architects need math? → Yes, strong basics required.

title: Psychologist
overview: Psychologists study human behavior and mental processes, helping people manage stress, trauma, and mental health issues.
work_profile: Counseling patients, conducting psychological tests, therapy sessions, research in human behavior.
roadmap: Jobs → Counselor → Psychologist → Senior Clinical Psychologist
duration: 3 years UG, 2 years PG, 3–5 years PhD (optional)
future_scope: Growing awareness of mental health is driving demand for psychologists globally.
day_in_life: Counseling sessions, therapy, research, interacting with patients.
faqs: Q: Can I practice with just a bachelor's? → No, minimum master's is required.
Q: Is psychology only for Arts students? → No, Science students can also pursue it.
